summary: Data quality audit of GOES fire confidence and RTMA weather data across all 28 fires.

goes_fire_confidence.note: Values are discrete confidence levels mapped from GOES mask codes, not continuous.

nan_pixels.note: July_Complex has 2.67% NaN; others <0.25%. NaN pixels are filtered by isfinite check in build_hour_samples.

zero_frame_dominance.note: Many fires have >50% all-zero timesteps (no fire detected anywhere in grid). These timesteps still generate training samples where label=0 everywhere. This massively inflates the negative class and means most training signal comes from 'no fire anywhere' hours.

issue_1_temperature_units.description: Temperature (TMP) is stored in Celsius, not Kelvin. Range across all fires: -23.78°C to 48.09°C. This is physically valid for California summer/fall fires. The model normalizes via z-score so the unit doesn't affect training, but downstream code or documentation expecting Kelvin will be wrong.
issue_1_temperature_units.action: Document that TMP is in Celsius. No code change needed since z-score normalization is unit-agnostic.

issue_2_precipitation_sentinel_9999.description: NOAA RTMA uses 9999.0 as a missing-data sentinel for precipitation (ACPC01). These values are NOT cleaned before entering the model. A single 9999 value means '9999 mm of rain in one hour' — obviously impossible (world record is ~300mm/hr). Worse: the discounted_rain_30d feature ACCUMULATES these, so one sentinel contaminates ~720 subsequent hours of the exponential decay feature.

affected_fires.Kincade: 5.75% of precip pixels are 9999 (18,954 pixels). Mean precip inflated to 575 mm/hr.
affected_fires.Walker: 0.34% of precip pixels are 9999 (1,458 pixels). Mean precip inflated to 33.8 mm/hr.
affected_fires.Red_Salmon_Complex: 0.06% of precip pixels are 9999 (2,379 pixels). Mean precip inflated to 5.5 mm/hr.
affected_fires.July_Complex: 0.03% of precip pixels are 9999 (416 pixels). Mean precip inflated to 3.4 mm/hr.

downstream_effects: precipitation_1h feature: 9999 value becomes ~67 standard deviations after z-score normalization
discounted_rain_30d feature: sentinel propagates through exponential decay, contaminating hundreds of subsequent timesteps
normalizer mean/std: inflated by sentinel values, distorting normalization for ALL fires
XGBoost: tree splits may learn to key on these outliers as spurious predictors
issue_2_precipitation_sentinel_9999.fix: Clamp ACPC01 to [0, 100] or replace values > 100 with 0.0 before feature engineering.

feature_pipeline.note: build_hour_samples and iter_aligned_hours_for_fire do not sanitize RTMA values. NaN is handled by np.isfinite filtering on the output, but 9999 sentinel values are finite and pass through.
feature_pipeline.discounted_rain_contamination: A 9999 sentinel at hour T propagates via exponential decay: at T+1 the discounted_rain is ~9999*decay, at T+168 (1 week) it's still ~4999. The lookback window is 720 hours (30 days), so contamination persists for a full month of subsequent timesteps.

class_balance.note: Only 0.29% of all pixels across all fires/hours are positive (confidence >= 0.1). After 3% negative subsampling, training positive rate is ~9.3%.

recommendations: CRITICAL: Clamp ACPC01 to [0, 100] in the RTMA loading pipeline to remove 9999 sentinels.
MEDIUM: Consider filtering out all-zero GOES frames to reduce negative class noise — or at minimum reducing their weight.
LOW: Document that TMP is in Celsius for future reference.
INVESTIGATION: The precipitation sentinel contamination may explain why weather features hurt the model — the model may be learning sentinel-driven patterns that don't generalize.
